title: Becoming a chatbot: my life as a real estate AI’s human backup – podcast
author: Written by Laura Preston and read by Erin Hunter. Produced by Jessica Beck. The executive producer was Ellie Bury
standfirst: For one weird year, I was the human who stepped in to make sure a property chatbot didn’t blow its cover – I was a person pretending to be a computer pretending to be a person. By Laura Preston
body_text: Read the text version here A longer version of this story appears in the latest n+1 magazine

people_mentioned.name: Laura Preston
people_mentioned.role: Author and narrator who worked as human backup for a real estate chatbot

cross_domain_bridges: This memoir bridges technology and labor economics by revealing how AI automation often relies on hidden human labor, demonstrating the economic reality that 'artificial intelligence' frequently masks low-wage human workers. The experience connects to psychology and sociology through its exploration of identity confusion when humans must perform as machines, raising questions about authenticity and the psychological toll of technological deception in modern work environments.

film_adaptation.pitch: A writer spends a year as the human backup for a property chatbot, forced to impersonate artificial intelligence when the AI fails — becoming a person pretending to be a computer pretending to be a person.
